LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT URBAN COMPASS, INC. DBA COMPASS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT URBAN COMPASS, INC.'S (D/B/A COMPASS) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED ON THE GROUND THAT IT HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. (CODE CIV. PROC., 437C, SUBD. (C); SEE MINISH V. HANUMAN FELLOWSHIP (2013) 154 CAL.APP.4TH 437, 444.) COMPASS'S ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IS ALSO DENIED. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Feb-24-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS URBAN COMPASS, INC. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Emblidge, Esq. 415-362-3599 of Moscone Emblidge Rubens for Plaintiff.
Stephen Hankins, Esq. 415-275-8550 of Riley Safer Holmes & Cancila LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues 2 major issues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT URBAN COMPASS, INC. DBA COMPASS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Urban Compass, Inc.'s (d/b/a Compass) motion for summary judgment is denied on the ground that it has not met its initial burden of production. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c, subd. (c); see Minish v. Hanuman Fellowship (2013) 154 Cal.App.4th 437, 444.) Compass's alternative motion for summary adjudication on the three causes of action is also denied. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) (see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT URBAN COMPASS, INC.'S APPLICATION OF JOHN KENNETH THEIS, TO BE ADMITTED AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE ON BEHALF OF URBAN COMPASS, INC.

The verified application of John Kenneth Theis to be admitted as counsel pro hac vice to Defendant Urban Compass, Inc. d/b/a Compass is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.
